data.title: XRP surges 22%, reaching $3.66 ATH as market cap hits $210B, driven by positive catalysts and investor confidence.
data.description: XRP surged to reach an all-time high of $3.66, buoyed by the approval of significant cryptocurrency legislations in the US House. Various positive factors contributed to boosting investor confidence in the cryptocurrency.

data.title: Dogecoin up 11%, trading volume surges by 64% to $5.56 billion; price reaches $0.24, weekly gains 20% and monthly gains 40%.
data.description: Dogecoin (CRYPTO: DOGE) experienced a significant surge, increasing by over 11% in the past 24 hours. Its trading volume also saw a notable uptick, rising by 64% to $5.56 billion. This surge propelled the coin above $0.24, a level it had not reached in almost five months. As a result, Dogecoin has accrued weekly gains of 20% and monthly gains of nearly 40%. Renowned cryptocurrency analyst and trader Ali Martinez highlighted that Dogecoin has surpassed the crucial $0.20 mark, indicating the next major obstacle at $0.36. The decision of a Nasdaq-listed firm to establish a reserve of Dogecoin contributed to this recent rally.

data.title: BitMine's stock surges 14% after hours as their Ethereum holdings surpass $1 billion post $250 million raise. Stock up 423.75% YTD.
data.description: BitMine Immersion Technologies Inc. (BMNR) experienced a significant surge of 14.11% in after-hours trading on Thursday, reaching $47.81 per share. This increase came as the company's Ethereum (ETH) holdings surpassed $1 billion. The Las Vegas-based company disclosed in an 8-K filing with the Securities and Exchange Commission that it currently holds 163,142 ETH tokens, valued at around $1.18 billion based on current prices. BitMine recently finalized a $250 million private placement, contributing to its substantial ETH holdings. The company's stock has shown remarkable growth, with a 423.75% increase year-to-date, elevating its market capitalization from $25.86 million earlier in the year to roughly $2.16 billion by Thursday's close. Prior to the after-hours surge, shares closed at $41.90, reflecting a 6.47% decrease from the previous session. The news has caught the attention of many investors and analysts, with notable figures like Tom Lee from Fundstrat recently appointed as the chairman.

data.title: Truist Financial Corp reported Q2 2025 results today, with earnings call scheduled at 8am ET. Details below.
data.description: Truist Financial Corporation (NYSE: TFC) announced its second quarter 2025 results in Charlotte, N.C. on July 18, 2025. Investors can tune in to the live earnings call for this quarter at 8 a.m. ET via webcast at app.webinar.net/z5gqlB9OVNL or by dialing 1-877-883-0383 with passcode 5911048. For more details, the earnings release and investor presentation are accessible.

data.title: Fintech companies plan to shift to DeFi lending in 3 years, notes Morpho co-founder, citing its decentralized appeal.
data.description: The co-founder of Morpho states that fintech companies are preparing to embrace DeFi lending because of its unrestricted nature.

data.title: Aurora Mobile's JPush partners with Beijing Hyundai Auto Finance to provide secure mobile financial services. (Characters: 94)
data.description: Shenzhen, China - July 18, 2025 - Aurora Mobile Limited (NASDAQ: JG), a prominent provider of customer engagement and marketing tech services in China, revealed a significant partnership for its push notification tool, JPush. The alliance is with Beijing Hyundai Auto Finance Co., Ltd. (“BHAF”), aimed at enhancing the automotive finance provider's capabilities. Through this collaboration, BHAF will benefit from JPush's effective message distribution and secure communication services.

data.title: CICC hosts the 3rd China–Southeast Asia Economic and Finance Forum in Singapore with 300 attendees, focusing on regional collaboration.
data.description: On July 15, 2025, the 3rd China-Southeast Asia Economic and Finance Forum 2025 was successfully organized by China International Capital Corporation Limited (CICC) in Singapore. The event was honored by the presence of H.E. Cao Zhongming, China's Ambassador to Singapore, who delivered a keynote address. The forum saw the participation of nearly 300 attendees including government officials, institutional investors, business leaders, and CICC representatives from various Asian regions. Themed "The Great Rewiring: China-Southeast Asia Pivot in a Realigning Global Economy," the forum provided a significant platform for discussions and partnerships on macroeconomic trends, regional investment prospects, and cross-border capital movements between China and Southeast Asia.
